input_text: Budak zaman sekarang tak tau bersyukur langsung. Mak bapak hantar belajar oversea pun still komplen wifi slow. Zaman kita dulu tulis surat je dah syukur.
reason: Generational stereotyping that dismisses younger generation's concerns as ingratitude. Paints all young Malaysians with one brush.
suggested_rewrite: Kadang-kadang saya rasa generasi muda mungkin kurang sedar betapa beruntungnya mereka. Harap mereka boleh menghargai peluang yang ada.

input_text: Gen Z Malaysia ni memang hopeless la. Suruh kerja sikit dah burnout. Cuba dulu zaman kita kerja 12 jam sehari takde cuti pun boleh survive.
reason: Dismisses mental health concerns of younger generation as weakness, stereotyping all Gen Z as lazy.
suggested_rewrite: Saya perasan budaya kerja dah berubah berbanding zaman dulu. Mungkin ada baiknya kita berbincang tentang keseimbangan kerja-kehidupan.

input_text: Orang Johor ni memang la, drive macam highway Malaysia semua bapak dia punya. Signal pun tak nak bagi.
reason: Negative stereotyping of drivers from Johor, attributing aggressive driving to an entire state's population.
suggested_rewrite: Saya pernah alami beberapa pemandu yang agak berbahaya di Johor. Harap semua pemandu boleh lebih berhati-hati di jalan raya.

input_text: Millennials asyik nak WFH je. Pantas la economy tak maju. Zaman dulu kita rajin pergi office, sebab tu negara boleh maju.
reason: Blames economic issues on millennials' work-from-home preference, ignoring legitimate workplace evolution.
suggested_rewrite: Ada pelbagai pendapat tentang WFH. Mungkin kita perlu cari keseimbangan antara kerja dari rumah dan di pejabat untuk produktiviti terbaik.

input_text: Orang Kelantan datang KL confirm cakap loghat kuat-kuat dalam LRT. Tak sedar ke orang lain pun ada? Memang la stereotype tu betul.
reason: Stereotypes Kelantanese people as loud and inconsiderate in public spaces, reinforcing negative regional prejudice.
suggested_rewrite: Kadang-kadang saya dengar pelbagai loghat di LRT. Sebenarnya ia menunjukkan kepelbagaian budaya Malaysia yang unik.

input_text: Sabah Sarawak punya orang selalu complain tak dapat development. Dah la jauh, nak demand macam-macam pulak. Bersyukur la masih dalam Malaysia.
reason: Dismisses legitimate development concerns from East Malaysia, implies they should be grateful rather than having equal rights to development.
suggested_rewrite: Pembangunan di Sabah dan Sarawak memang penting dan perlu dipertingkatkan. Mereka berhak mendapat kemudahan yang setara.

input_text: Boss kata 'fresh grad tak boleh demand tinggi-tinggi'. Tapi gaji dia naik 30% tahun ni. Hypocrite betul la boomer-boomer ni.
reason: While expressing legitimate workplace frustration, resorts to generational name-calling ('boomer') as an insult.
suggested_rewrite: Saya rasa tidak adil apabila pekerja baru diminta terima gaji rendah sedangkan pihak pengurusan menikmati kenaikan gaji yang besar. Perlu ada keadilan dalam pampasan.

input_text: Dah la orang Kedah slow je buat kerja. Nasib baik nasi kandar sedap, kalau tak memang takde reason nak pergi sana.
reason: Stereotypes people from Kedah as slow workers, reducing the state's value to just its food.
suggested_rewrite: Kedah mempunyai banyak tarikan termasuk makanan yang sedap. Saya harap dapat melawat dan mengenali budaya mereka dengan lebih baik.

input_text: Jiran sebelah buat renovation sampai malam. Hari Deepavali pulak tu mercun bunyi sampai pukul 2 pagi. Memang tak consider orang lain langsung kan.
reason: Ties Deepavali celebrations to general inconsideration, implying the religious celebration itself is a nuisance.
suggested_rewrite: Bunyi bising dari renovation dan mercun lewat malam memang mengganggu. Mungkin boleh berbincang dengan jiran tentang waktu yang sesuai.

input_text: Orang Terengganu datang KL kerja pastu komplen mahal. Balik la kampung kalau tak mampu. Tak ada siapa paksa datang sini.
reason: Dismisses legitimate cost-of-living concerns and tells people from other states to 'go back', showing regional hostility.
suggested_rewrite: Kos sara hidup di KL memang tinggi dan ia cabaran bagi ramai orang dari pelbagai negeri. Kerajaan perlu pertimbangkan penyelesaian yang lebih baik.

input_text: Intern sekarang first day dah tanya pasal WFH policy. Bro belum buat apa-apa dah nak privilege. Zaman kita dulu datang awal balik lambat takde komplen.
reason: Mocks new interns for asking about flexible work, dismissing reasonable workplace questions as entitlement.
suggested_rewrite: Pekerja baru zaman sekarang lebih peka tentang work-life balance. Kita boleh bimbing mereka sambil menghormati keperluan mereka.

input_text: Setiap kali balik kampung, makcik-makcik mesti tanya 'bila nak kahwin'. Annoying gila. Orang kampung memang takde benda lain nak cakap ke?
reason: While the frustration about personal questions is valid, it stereotypes rural relatives as having nothing meaningful to discuss.
suggested_rewrite: Kadang-kadang soalan peribadi dari saudara-mara boleh terasa agak menekan. Tapi saya faham mereka tanya kerana prihatin.

input_text: Budak sekarang order grab food je hari-hari. Masak pun tak reti. Pastu komplen duit tak cukup. Common sense pun takde ke?
reason: Stereotypes young people as financially irresponsible and lacking basic life skills.
suggested_rewrite: Perkhidmatan penghantaran makanan memang mudah tapi boleh jadi mahal. Mungkin ada baiknya belajar masak untuk menjimatkan wang.

input_text: Mak cik WhatsApp group forward berita palsu lagi. Boomer memang senang kena scam. Technology bukan untuk semua orang la.
reason: Stereotypes older Malaysians as gullible with technology, using 'boomer' dismissively.
suggested_rewrite: Penyebaran berita palsu di WhatsApp memang membimbangkan. Mungkin kita boleh bantu ahli keluarga yang kurang celik digital untuk mengenal pasti maklumat palsu.

input_text: Typical la, suruh budak Gen Z buat presentation sikit dah anxiety. Kita dulu kena marah depan whole office pun ok je. Lemah betul generasi ni.
reason: Dismisses anxiety as weakness, normalizes past workplace abuse, and stereotypes an entire generation.
suggested_rewrite: Kebimbangan semasa pembentangan adalah perkara biasa. Kita boleh bantu pekerja muda membina keyakinan dengan sokongan yang betul.

input_text: Orang Pahang memang relax sangat. Nak siap satu project pun ambil masa berbulan. Patut la negeri tak maju macam Selangor.
reason: Stereotypes Pahang residents as lazy and compares states unfavorably, dismissing a whole population.
suggested_rewrite: Setiap negeri mempunyai kekuatan tersendiri. Pembangunan bergantung pada banyak faktor, bukan sikap penduduk semata-mata.

input_text: Fresh grad nak gaji RM4k tapi English pun tunggang langgang. Resume siap ada spelling error. Ini ke future leaders kita?
reason: Mocks fresh graduates' English proficiency while dismissing their potential, using sarcasm to question their capabilities.
suggested_rewrite: Kemahiran bahasa Inggeris memang penting dalam pasaran kerja. Mungkin lebih banyak program pembangunan bahasa diperlukan di peringkat universiti.

input_text: Lepas ni confirm la developer buat rumah low cost sebelah taman kita. Habis la property value. Macam-macam jenis orang masuk nanti.
reason: Frames low-cost housing residents as undesirable 'types of people', showing class-based prejudice disguised as property concern.
suggested_rewrite: Pembangunan rumah mampu milik penting untuk masyarakat. Harap pihak pemaju akan merancang infrastruktur yang mencukupi untuk semua penduduk.

input_text: Jiran India saya tiap-tiap pagi pasang lagu kuil kuat gila. Aku dah pindah rumah pun masih kena dengar. Tak boleh ke pakai earphone?
reason: Dismisses devotional music as mere noise, with sarcastic suggestion that implies religious practice should be hidden.
suggested_rewrite: Bunyi muzik dari jiran kadang-kadang agak kuat terutama pada waktu pagi. Mungkin saya boleh berbincang secara baik dengan mereka tentang tahap bunyi.

input_text: Anak zaman sekarang makan pun nak Instagram dulu. Makanan dah sejuk baru makan. Priorities memang salah betul generation ni.
reason: Trivializes younger generation's social media habits as wrong priorities, dismissing their cultural norms.
suggested_rewrite: Budaya berkongsi gambar makanan di media sosial memang berbeza daripada kebiasaan lama. Setiap generasi ada cara menikmati hidup masing-masing.

input_text: Pekerja Bangladesh kat site ni memang slow. Bagi kerja sikit pun tak faham. Susah la macam ni nak siapkan project on time.
reason: Stereotypes migrant workers as slow and incompetent, showing disrespect for foreign workers' dignity.
suggested_rewrite: Komunikasi dengan pekerja asing kadang-kadang mencabar kerana halangan bahasa. Mungkin kita perlu sediakan arahan yang lebih jelas atau penterjemah.

input_text: Aku memang tak suka kerja dengan orang yang datang dari kampung. Mindset sempit, teknologi pun tak reti guna. Lambat sangat nak adapt.
reason: Stereotypes rural Malaysians as narrow-minded and technologically illiterate, expressing workplace prejudice.
suggested_rewrite: Sesetengah rakan sekerja mungkin memerlukan lebih masa untuk menyesuaikan diri dengan teknologi baru. Kita boleh bantu mereka melalui latihan dan sokongan.

input_text: Budak TikTok sekarang buat content bodoh-bodoh pastu viral. Ini ke harapan negara? Dulu kita belajar rajin-rajin, sekarang menari depan phone pun boleh kaya.
reason: Dismisses content creation as 'stupid' and questions the potential of the younger generation based on their chosen medium.
suggested_rewrite: Media sosial telah membuka peluang kerjaya baru untuk generasi muda. Walaupun ada content yang kurang bermakna, ramai juga yang menghasilkan kandungan yang kreatif dan bermanfaat.

input_text: HR department semua perempuan. Pantas la semua keputusan emotional. Kena ada balance la baru objective.
reason: Associates women-led departments with emotional decision-making, perpetuating gender stereotypes in the workplace.
suggested_rewrite: Kepelbagaian dalam jabatan memang penting untuk perspektif yang lebih luas. Setiap keputusan HR perlu berdasarkan fakta dan polisi syarikat.

input_text: Orang Negeri Sembilan cakap pelik sangat. 'Den', 'ghoman' semua tu. KL orang dengar pun tak faham. Zaman dah moden, cakap BM standard la.
reason: Mocks Negeri Sembilan dialect as weird and suggests they should abandon it, showing disrespect for linguistic heritage.
suggested_rewrite: Dialek Negeri Sembilan adalah warisan budaya yang unik. Kepelbagaian bahasa dan loghat memperkayakan identiti Malaysia.

input_text: Baru masuk office dah cerita pasal mental health. Gen Z ni betul-betul snowflake. Cuba kena kerja kilang macam atuk nenek diorang dulu.
reason: Uses 'snowflake' label to dismiss mental health discussions, comparing modern workplace stress to factory labour dismissively.
suggested_rewrite: Kesedaran tentang kesihatan mental di tempat kerja semakin meningkat dan ia sesuatu yang positif. Setiap generasi menghadapi cabaran yang berbeza.

input_text: Kalau bukan sebab subsidi kerajaan, orang kampung mana boleh hidup. Asyik harap bantuan je. Bila nak berdikari?
reason: Stereotypes rural Malaysians as dependent on government aid, dismissing structural economic challenges they face.
suggested_rewrite: Program bantuan kerajaan memang membantu rakyat luar bandar. Pada masa yang sama, pembangunan ekonomi luar bandar perlu diperkukuhkan agar mereka berpeluang untuk maju.

input_text: Sekolah Tamil sebelah rumah bunyi loceng kuat sangat pagi-pagi. Dah la anak aku tak sekolah situ. Asal kami kena dengar?
reason: Frames a Tamil school bell as nuisance specifically because the commenter's child doesn't attend it, implying only personal relevance matters.
suggested_rewrite: Bunyi loceng sekolah pada waktu pagi kadang-kadang agak kuat. Mungkin boleh berbincang dengan pihak sekolah tentang tahap bunyi yang sesuai.

input_text: Projek kerajaan lambat siap sebab bagi kat kontraktor kroni. Orang biasa macam kita kerja kuat tapi orang atasan senang-senang je dapat duit. System memang rosak.
reason: While government project delays are legitimate concerns, broad accusations of cronyism without evidence foster distrust.
suggested_rewrite: Kelewatan projek kerajaan memang membimbangkan. Proses perolehan kerajaan perlu lebih telus dan akauntabel untuk memastikan projek siap tepat pada masanya.

input_text: Orang Melaka memang la, semua benda nak claim 'original'. Laksa pun kata Melaka punya paling sedap. State kecik tapi ego besar.
reason: Mocks Melaka's cultural pride and reduces an entire state's population to a negative stereotype.
suggested_rewrite: Melaka mempunyai warisan budaya yang kaya dan unik. Setiap negeri berhak berbangga dengan keistimewaan masing-masing.

input_text: Sape suruh beli rumah kat kawasan Melayu kalau tak suka azan. Dah tau Malaysia negara Islam, nak komplen apa lagi? Balik la negara asal.
reason: Uses religion to shut down dialogue and tells fellow citizens to 'go back to their country of origin', a hurtful trope.
suggested_rewrite: Malaysia memang negara yang mengutamakan Islam tetapi juga menghormati semua agama. Kita boleh berbincang tentang isu bunyi bising dengan cara yang saling menghormati.

input_text: Gen Z nak kerja remote je. Come to office sikit pun tak sanggup. Macam mana nak bina networking kalau depan laptop je 24/7?
reason: Stereotypes Gen Z as unwilling to work in offices, dismissing remote work as inferior without nuance.
suggested_rewrite: Kerja remote ada kelebihan dan kekurangan. Networking memang penting dan boleh dilakukan secara hybrid untuk keseimbangan terbaik.

input_text: Budak sekarang jumpa masalah sikit dah nak give up. Nak viral je pandai. Kerja betul-betul tak reti. Entah apa yang sekolah ajar.
reason: Stereotypes young people as quitters who only know social media, while also dismissing the education system.
suggested_rewrite: Ketahanan menghadapi cabaran adalah kemahiran penting. Mungkin sistem pendidikan dan tempat kerja boleh menyediakan lebih banyak sokongan untuk pembangunan kemahiran ini.

input_text: Mana boleh buat kuil kat sini, kawasan ni majority Melayu. Nanti satu kawasan jadi lain macam. Pergi la buat kat tempat lain.
reason: Objects to a temple based on the area's racial composition, implying religious buildings change the character of an area negatively.
suggested_rewrite: Pembinaan rumah ibadat perlu melalui proses perancangan yang betul. Semua komuniti berhak mempunyai tempat ibadat, dan perbincangan terbuka dengan penduduk boleh membantu.

input_text: Jiran Melayu aku tiap kali kenduri parking kereta depan gate aku. Dah cakap banyak kali tapi buat bodoh je. Memang la typical.
reason: Identifies the neighbour's race unnecessarily when the issue is parking, implying racial character flaw with 'typical'.
suggested_rewrite: Jiran saya sering parking depan gate saya semasa kenduri. Saya akan cuba berbincang sekali lagi atau hubungi pihak berkuasa tempatan untuk bantuan.

input_text: These young graduates expect RM5k starting salary but can't even write a proper email. Back in my day we started at RM1.2k and worked our way up without complaining.
reason: Dismisses graduates' salary expectations while stereotyping them as incompetent, ignoring inflation and cost of living changes.
suggested_rewrite: Starting salary expectations have changed over the years. It would help if graduates also focused on building strong communication skills alongside their academic qualifications.

input_text: My neighbor keeps burning incense every morning until my whole house smells. I get it's your culture but have some consideration for others la. So inconsiderate.
reason: While the concern about smoke is valid, the framing dismisses cultural/religious practice as mere inconsideration.
suggested_rewrite: The incense smoke from next door sometimes comes into my house. I'd like to discuss this politely with my neighbour to find a solution that works for both of us.

input_text: Typical Penang aunty, queue so long still want to cut queue. No wonder people say Penang people kiasu.
reason: Uses a queue-cutting incident to reinforce negative stereotypes about Penang people, combined with ageist framing.
suggested_rewrite: I saw someone cutting the queue today which was frustrating. I wish everyone would be more considerate in public spaces.

input_text: Why ah every time Chinese New Year the lion dance must be so loud? Some of us working from home leh. Not everyone celebrates your festival ok.
reason: Complains about Chinese New Year lion dance noise in a way that dismisses cultural celebrations as mere disturbances.
suggested_rewrite: Lion dances are a lovely tradition but the noise can be challenging for those working from home. Perhaps organisers could coordinate timing with residents.

input_text: These property developers want to build a temple right next to our housing area. Great, more noise pollution and traffic. Just what we needed.
reason: Frames a religious building as purely a source of noise and traffic, showing cultural insensitivity disguised as development concern.
suggested_rewrite: I have concerns about the traffic impact of the proposed new building near our housing area. Perhaps we could discuss mitigation plans with the developer and community.

input_text: Perak people always so proud of their ipoh white coffee but the whole state got nothing else to offer la. Even young people all run to KL already.
reason: Dismisses Perak's contributions beyond coffee, insulting the state's development and opportunities.
suggested_rewrite: Perak is famous for its white coffee and has many other attractions too. Brain drain is a challenge many states face and needs to be addressed collectively.

input_text: Every Hari Raya my Malay neighbor's open house so many cars block the whole road. Very inconsiderate. They only think about themselves.
reason: Frames Hari Raya celebrations as selfish behavior, showing intolerance for cultural traditions.
suggested_rewrite: During festive open houses, parking can sometimes be an issue on our street. Maybe we can work together to coordinate parking arrangements.

input_text: Millennials and their avocado toast. No wonder can't afford house in KL. We saved every sen and bought property by 25. This generation just knows how to complain.
reason: Uses the avocado toast trope to dismiss legitimate housing affordability concerns, ignoring changed economic conditions.
suggested_rewrite: Housing affordability is a real challenge today. Property prices have risen much faster than wages compared to previous decades, making it harder for young Malaysians.

input_text: Why do Malay weddings always have to block the whole street? Canopy everywhere, parking gone. Other people also live here la.
reason: Targets Malay wedding traditions as inherently inconsiderate, rather than addressing specific logistical issues constructively.
suggested_rewrite: Large celebrations sometimes cause parking and access challenges in residential areas. It would help if organisers coordinated with neighbours in advance.

input_text: Sarawak colleague always late for meeting. When I say something, she say 'Sarawak time ma'. Since when got different time zone in Malaysia?
reason: Uses a colleague's lateness to mock Sarawak culture, implying people from East Malaysia are inherently unpunctual.
suggested_rewrite: Punctuality is important for meetings regardless of where we're from. Perhaps we can discuss expectations around meeting times as a team.

input_text: Another day another azan complaint from my condo WhatsApp group. You live in Malaysia la bro, what did you expect? Go migrate if you don't like it.
reason: While defending the azan, uses aggressive 'go migrate' rhetoric to shut down discussion rather than fostering understanding.
suggested_rewrite: The azan is an important part of Malaysian culture and protected by law. If the volume is a concern, perhaps the management could help mediate a respectful discussion.

input_text: Every year same story. Thaipusam procession block the road, then everybody stuck in jam for hours. They cannot do it somewhere else ah?
reason: Frames Thaipusam procession as merely a traffic inconvenience, dismissing its deep religious significance.
suggested_rewrite: Thaipusam is an important Hindu festival. While road closures can be inconvenient, we can plan our routes in advance. It's part of Malaysia's multicultural heritage.

input_text: Our condo wants to build a Chinese school bus stop right at the entrance. Confirm traffic jam every morning la. Why can't they use their own school compound?
reason: Specifically identifies the school as 'Chinese' when the concern is traffic, adding unnecessary racial framing to a development issue.
suggested_rewrite: There's a proposal for a school bus stop at our condo entrance. I'm concerned about traffic. Perhaps we can discuss alternative locations with the management.

input_text: Malaysian Chinese always complain about everything but never want to integrate. Speak Mandarin only, eat at Chinese restaurant only. Then complain about unity.
reason: Stereotypes Malaysian Chinese as insular and blames them for lack of unity, ignoring the complexity of multicultural society.
suggested_rewrite: National unity requires effort from all communities. Many Malaysians of all backgrounds socialise and work together daily. We can all do more to build cross-cultural connections.

input_text: I told my Indian colleague his curry smells too strong in the office and he got offended. So sensitive la these people, cannot take feedback.
reason: Identifies colleague by race when discussing food complaints, then labels the entire group as oversensitive.
suggested_rewrite: Strong food smells in the office can be challenging for some colleagues. Perhaps we can establish shared guidelines about food in common areas.

input_text: These Grab drivers from Myanmar cannot even speak BM properly. How they got license also I don't know. Dangerous la like this.
reason: Targets Myanmar drivers' language skills to question their competence, showing bias against migrant workers.
suggested_rewrite: Language barriers with some ride-hailing drivers can be challenging for navigation. Perhaps ride-hailing companies could provide basic BM training for drivers.

input_text: KL people come Penang, first thing they say 'wah so cheap'. Ya la you all overpay for everything in KL, that's your problem la.
reason: Passive-aggressively mocks KL visitors' reactions to Penang prices, fostering inter-state rivalry.
suggested_rewrite: Cost of living varies across Malaysian states. Both KL and Penang have their own advantages when it comes to affordability and opportunities.

input_text: I work so hard as account executive but my Malay colleague who does less got promoted because of you-know-what la. Meritocracy my foot.
reason: Implies a Malay colleague's promotion was due to racial quotas rather than merit, using coded language to suggest racial favouritism.
suggested_rewrite: I feel the promotion criteria should be more transparent. It would help if management communicated clearly what performance metrics are used for advancement.

input_text: Young people nowadays spend more on bubble tea than savings. Then cry about not being able to afford BTO. Whose fault is that?
reason: Uses the bubble tea spending trope to victim-blame young people for housing affordability issues.
suggested_rewrite: Financial literacy is important for young adults. However, housing affordability is a systemic issue that goes beyond individual spending habits.

input_text: My Malay neighbour asked me to lower my Chinese New Year music. But during Raya their kompang so loud nobody says anything. Double standard much?
reason: Pits Chinese New Year celebrations against Hari Raya, creating a racial/religious comparison that fosters resentment.
suggested_rewrite: Noise during festive celebrations affects everyone. Perhaps our neighbourhood could agree on reasonable hours for festive music regardless of the occasion.

input_text: Taxi uncle in KL always try to cheat tourist with no meter. Then they wonder why everyone use Grab now. Padan muka la.
reason: Stereotypes all KL taxi drivers as cheats and expresses schadenfreude at their decline, lacking empathy.
suggested_rewrite: Some taxi drivers in KL have been known to overcharge, which has contributed to the rise of ride-hailing apps. The industry needs better regulation to protect both drivers and passengers.

input_text: Every time got meeting, the Malay colleagues sure take prayer break. Meeting already running late, still want to stop. Efficiency zero la like this.
reason: Frames prayer breaks as an efficiency problem, showing intolerance for religious obligations in the workplace.
suggested_rewrite: Meeting schedules could be better planned around prayer times so that everyone can participate fully without feeling rushed.

input_text: Local council approved a low-cost flat project near our bungalow area. Sure depreciate our property value. What kind of people will move in, you can imagine la.
reason: Uses coded language ('what kind of people') to express classist prejudice against low-income residents.
suggested_rewrite: The new housing project near our area raises some concerns about infrastructure capacity. I hope the council has planned adequate amenities for all residents.

input_text: Chinese New Year lah, Raya lah, Deepavali lah. Malaysia too many holidays already. No wonder productivity low compared to Singapore.
reason: Blames Malaysia's multicultural holidays for productivity issues, implying diversity is a burden.
suggested_rewrite: Malaysia's multicultural holidays are a unique part of our national identity. Productivity depends on many factors beyond the number of public holidays.

input_text: Gated community kita nak buat playground tapi some residents want surau instead. Not everyone is Muslim la. Think about all residents please.
reason: Frames a surau request as inconsiderate of non-Muslims, rather than seeking a balanced solution for all.
suggested_rewrite: Both a playground and a surau serve important community needs. Perhaps the residents' committee can explore options that accommodate different requirements.

input_text: 现在的年轻人啊，整天只会刷手机。叫他们帮忙做家务就说累。我们那个年代哪有这样的。
reason: Stereotypes all young people as phone-addicted and lazy regarding household chores, dismissing generational differences.
suggested_rewrite: 现在的年轻人和我们的生活方式不同。也许我们可以互相理解，一起分担家务。

input_text: 每次开斋节放鞭炮到半夜，吵死人了。也不想想别人要上班的。自己开心就好是吗？
reason: Complains about Hari Raya celebrations in a way that dismisses the cultural significance and frames celebrants as selfish.
suggested_rewrite: 开斋节庆祝活动很热闹，但深夜的鞭炮声可能会影响邻居休息。希望大家可以在合理的时间内庆祝。

input_text: 槟城人就是这样的啦，什么都要跟KL比。自己一个小岛，ego比双峰塔还高。
reason: Mocks Penang residents' perceived sense of pride, reducing them to a stereotype of having oversized egos.
suggested_rewrite: 槟城和KL各有各的特色和优势。两个地方都为马来西亚的发展做出了贡献。

input_text: 吉隆坡的人就是看不起小地方来的。你问路他们也不想理你，好像你欠他们钱一样。
reason: Stereotypes all KL residents as arrogant and dismissive toward people from smaller towns.
suggested_rewrite: 大城市的节奏比较快，有时候人们可能显得比较冷淡。但我相信大多数KL人都是友善的。

input_text: 马来人的婚礼真的很夸张，整条路都封了。我们华人结婚哪里有这样的。有点公德心好不好。
reason: Negatively compares Malay wedding customs with Chinese ones, implying Malay traditions lack civic consciousness.
suggested_rewrite: 不同文化的婚礼习俗各有特色。如果婚礼影响了交通，也许可以提前和邻居协调。

input_text: 现在的马来西亚年轻人都跑去新加坡赚钱，留下来的都是没本事的。难怪国家发展慢。
reason: Insults Malaysians who choose to stay in the country as incompetent, while oversimplifying the brain drain issue.
suggested_rewrite: 人才外流确实是马来西亚面临的挑战。我们应该思考如何创造更好的机会，吸引人才留在国内。

input_text: 华人新年放鞭炮还可以理解，但连续放一个星期就过分了吧。有些人要工作的好不好。太自私了。
reason: Labels extended Chinese New Year celebrations as selfish, showing limited tolerance for cultural festivities.
suggested_rewrite: 农历新年是重要的传统节日。如果鞭炮声持续太久，也许社区可以商量一个大家都能接受的时间范围。

input_text: 印度同事每次开会都迟到。Indian time果然是真的。我们华人就比较准时。
reason: Uses a colleague's tardiness to reinforce racial stereotypes about punctuality, making unfair racial comparisons.
suggested_rewrite: 准时开会对所有人都很重要。如果同事经常迟到，可以私下提醒或和团队讨论会议纪律。

input_text: 这些马来年轻人整天在mamak坐到半夜。不用上班的吗？难怪经济不好。
reason: Stereotypes young Malays as idle mamak loiterers and blames them for economic issues, combining racial and generational prejudice.
suggested_rewrite: Mamak文化是马来西亚的特色之一。年轻人的社交方式和我们不同，不一定代表他们不努力工作。

input_text: 这里的印度邻居每次Deepavali放鞭炮到凌晨两三点。投诉也没用，management不敢管。偏心。
reason: Complains about Deepavali celebrations with accusation that management is biased, adding racial tension to a noise complaint.
suggested_rewrite: 深夜放鞭炮确实会影响到邻居。建议大家在合理的时间内庆祝，管理层也应该制定统一的规定。

input_text: 柔佛人什么都想跟新加坡比。拜托，你们又不是新加坡人。先把自己的州管好再说吧。
reason: Mocks Johor residents for comparing themselves to Singapore, dismissing their aspirations and state pride.
suggested_rewrite: 柔佛靠近新加坡，受到一些影响是自然的。每个州都有自己的发展目标和特色。

input_text: 我们这里要建一个印度庙。说真的，每次festival那个loudspeaker吵到不行。现在还要建更近，真的受不了。
reason: Objects to a Hindu temple primarily because of festival noise, framing religious celebrations as unbearable nuisances.
suggested_rewrite: 新庙宇的建设需要考虑周围居民的感受。也许可以通过社区对话找到大家都能接受的方案。

input_text: 这些外劳占了我们的工作机会。本地人找不到工是因为老板都想请便宜的外劳。
reason: Scapegoats migrant workers for local unemployment, ignoring economic complexities and employer responsibilities.
suggested_rewrite: 就业市场的问题涉及很多因素。政府需要制定公平的政策，既保护本地工人的权益，也尊重外劳的尊严。

input_text: 华人家长就是这样，什么都要补习。孩子累得要死也不管。只要成绩好就行。怪不得孩子压力那么大。
reason: Stereotypes Chinese Malaysian parents as obsessive about academic results, shaming their parenting approach.
suggested_rewrite: 过度补习确实可能给孩子带来压力。家长和教育者可以一起探讨更平衡的学习方式。

input_text: 马来同事每次到祈祷时间就消失半个小时。一天五次，加起来多少时间了？公平吗？
reason: Calculates prayer time as lost productivity, framing religious obligations as unfair workplace advantage.
suggested_rewrite: 祈祷时间是受法律保护的权利。如果对工作安排有疑问，可以和人事部讨论如何更好地协调时间表。

input_text: இந்த generation பிள்ளைகள் எதுக்கும் உதவாது. Phone-ல மட்டும் busy. நம்ம காலத்தில் இப்படி இருந்தா அப்பா அடி வாங்குவோம்.
reason: Stereotypes younger generation as useless and phone-addicted, while also normalizing physical punishment from the past.
suggested_rewrite: இன்றைய இளைஞர்கள் தொழில்நுட்பத்தில் திறமையானவர்கள். நாம் அவர்களுடன் பேசி புரிந்துகொள்ள முயற்சிக்கலாம்.

input_text: கோவிலுக்கு பக்கத்தில் வீடு வாங்கின, இப்ப loudspeaker சத்தம் தாங்க முடியல. யாருக்கும் அக்கறை இல்ல.
reason: Frames temple loudspeaker as pure nuisance without acknowledging its religious significance or seeking dialogue.
suggested_rewrite: கோவில் அருகில் வசிப்பது சில நேரங்களில் சத்தமாக இருக்கும். கோவில் நிர்வாகத்துடன் பேசி ஒரு நல்ல தீர்வு காணலாம்.

input_text: இந்த area-ல ஒரு surau கட்டப்போறாங்க. Parking already not enough, now sure more problem. யாரும் residents-கிட்ட கேக்கல.
reason: Frames construction of a prayer space primarily as a parking problem, dismissing religious needs of the community.
suggested_rewrite: புதிய surau கட்டுமானத்தால் parking பிரச்சனை ஏற்படலாம். இது பற்றி குடியிருப்பாளர்கள் கூட்டத்தில் பேசலாம்.

input_text: இந்த area-ல Chinese temple festival வரும்போது fireworks midnight வரை. நாங்க என்ன பாவம் செஞ்சோம்? Sleep also cannot sleep properly.
reason: Frames Chinese temple festival celebrations as punishment inflicted on neighbours, showing intolerance.
suggested_rewrite: கோவில் திருவிழா நேரத்தில் இரவு நேர பட்டாசு சத்தம் தூக்கத்தை பாதிக்கும். கோவில் நிர்வாகத்துடன் நேரம் குறித்து பேசலாம்.

input_text: எங்க office-ல ஒரு Chinese colleague lunch-க்கு pork சாப்பிட்டு வந்து microwave use பண்றார். Smellக்கு யாருக்கும் consideration இல்ல.
reason: Frames a colleague's food choice as inconsiderate, adding racial identifier unnecessarily and creating cultural tension.
suggested_rewrite: Office microwave-ல சில உணவுகளின் வாசனை மற்றவர்களுக்கு இடையூறாக இருக்கலாம். Office-ல microwave usage guidelines வைக்கலாம்.

input_text: எங்க area-ல ஒரு Malay family pindah masuk. இப்ப every subuh azan alarm full volume. முன்னாடி இந்த area quiet-ஆ இருந்தது.
reason: Frames a Muslim family's azan alarm as disrupting the area's peace, with undertone of not wanting them in the neighborhood.
suggested_rewrite: புதிய குடும்பம் வந்திருக்கிறது. சில நேரங்களில் காலை alarm சத்தமாக இருக்கும். நட்புடன் பேசி சரி செய்யலாம்.

input_text: இங்க ஒரு Chinese shop owner Tamil-ல பேசினா முகம் சுளிக்கிறார். Malaysia-ல இருக்கீங்க, எல்லா language-யும் respect பண்ணுங்க.
reason: Stereotypes Chinese shop owners as dismissive of Tamil language, though the underlying concern about language respect is valid.
suggested_rewrite: மொழி தடை சில நேரங்களில் தவறான புரிதலை ஏற்படுத்தும். பொறுமையாக communicate செய்வது நல்லது.

input_text: நம்ம area-ல lot of Malay families moved in. Now every Friday prayer time road all blocked. முன்னாடி parking problem இல்ல.
reason: Frames the arrival of Malay families and Friday prayers as the cause of parking problems, showing demographic unease.
suggested_rewrite: Friday prayer நேரத்தில் parking சிரமமாக இருக்கும். ஒழுங்குபடுத்தப்பட்ட parking arrangements செய்யலாம்.

input_text: இந்த company-ல Malay manager எல்லாருக்கும் bias. Chinese staff-க்கு bonus அதிகம் தருவார், நாம Tamil-க்கு எப்பவும் குறைவு.
reason: Makes racial accusations about bonus distribution without evidence, fostering inter-ethnic workplace tension.
suggested_rewrite: Bonus வழங்குவது transparent-ஆக இருக்க வேண்டும். HR-உடன் bonus criteria பற்றி பேசுவது நல்லது.

input_text: வெளிநாட்டு workers எல்லாம் dirty-ஆ இருக்காங்க. Bus-ல அவங்க பக்கத்தில் யாரும் உக்காற மாட்டாங்க. அவங்க country-க்கு போகட்டும்.
reason: Describes migrant workers as dirty and suggests they should leave, showing dehumanizing attitudes.
suggested_rewrite: வெளிநாட்டு தொழிலாளர்கள் நம் நாட்டின் வளர்ச்சிக்கு முக்கிய பங்காற்றுகிறார்கள். அவர்களுக்கு சரியான வசதிகள் வழங்குவது முதலாளிகளின் கடமை.

input_text: KL people always think they're better than everyone else la. Come to Penang also want to complain food not good enough. Balik la your overpriced city.
reason: Regional stereotyping pitting KL residents against Penang residents, with dismissive hostility.
suggested_rewrite: Food preferences differ from state to state. It's nice that we all have our own local favourites to be proud of.

input_text: Penang drivers are the worst in Malaysia la, no cap. Every time I go there sure kena honk for no reason. Island mentality betul.
reason: Negatively stereotypes Penang drivers and attributes it to an 'island mentality', generalizing an entire population.
suggested_rewrite: I've had some stressful driving experiences in Penang. Traffic culture can vary a lot between states.

input_text: Company hiring only wants Chinese speakers for front desk. Not racist ke? Oh wait, it's 'business requirement' la konon.
reason: Highlights a potentially discriminatory hiring practice but frames it with sarcasm rather than constructive criticism.
suggested_rewrite: I noticed some job listings require specific language skills that may not be essential for the role. This could unintentionally exclude qualified candidates from different backgrounds.

input_text: Confirm la orang JB weekend masuk Singapore beli barang murah pastu Monday komplen pasal ringgit jatuh. Self-awareness zero.
reason: Stereotypes Johor Bahru residents' cross-border shopping habits and mocks their economic awareness.
suggested_rewrite: Ramai rakyat Malaysia di kawasan sempadan memang memanfaatkan kadar pertukaran. Ia sebenarnya keputusan ekonomi yang rasional.

input_text: Condo management nak buat surau dalam compound. Haiya, property value sure drop one. Next time masjid pulak.
reason: Associates Islamic prayer spaces with property value decline, showing religious insensitivity disguised as property concern.
suggested_rewrite: Ada cadangan untuk membina surau di dalam kawasan kondo. Mungkin pihak pengurusan boleh adakan perbincangan terbuka dengan semua penghuni tentang reka bentuk dan lokasi yang sesuai.

input_text: Colleague kena promoted sebab dia pandai bodek boss. Kerja pun biasa je. Welcome to Malaysian corporate culture where talent doesn't matter.
reason: Dismisses a colleague's promotion as purely due to flattery, cynically generalizing Malaysian corporate culture.
suggested_rewrite: Kadang-kadang kenaikan pangkat mungkin tidak nampak adil dari luar. Tapi mungkin ada faktor lain yang kita tak tahu. Penting untuk fokus pada pembangunan kerjaya sendiri.

input_text: Penang lang really think their char kuey teow is the best in the world. Overrated gila. Come try Ipoh one first la before talk so big.
reason: Dismisses Penang food culture as overrated while elevating Ipoh's, fueling inter-state food rivalry with condescension.
suggested_rewrite: Malaysia has amazing food from every state. Both Penang and Ipoh have their own unique versions of char kuey teow worth trying.

input_text: My boss said 'we are like a family here' then rejected my MC. Typical Malaysian SME la, treat workers like robots but expect loyalty. Bodoh betul.
reason: While the workplace concern is valid, using 'bodoh' (stupid) as a personal insult crosses into rudeness.
suggested_rewrite: My boss rejected my MC despite promoting a 'family' work culture. Malaysian SMEs need to respect workers' rights to medical leave.

input_text: Condo aku nak buat tokong kecik kat bawah. Haiya, satu lantai nanti bau colok. Boleh ke kita petition?
reason: Objects to a small shrine in the condo specifically because of incense smell, framing religious practice as a nuisance to petition against.
suggested_rewrite: Ada cadangan untuk membina ruang ibadat kecil di kondo. Mungkin pihak pengurusan boleh adakan perbincangan tentang pengudaraan dan lokasi yang sesuai untuk semua penghuni.

input_text: Kat kampung sana still burn sampah openly. Orang kampung memang takde kesedaran alam sekitar. Educated people in city won't do this la.
reason: Equates open burning with rural ignorance, creating a false city-educated vs kampung-uneducated dichotomy.
suggested_rewrite: Open burning is still practiced in some rural areas, often due to lack of waste management services. Better waste collection infrastructure is needed in these communities.

input_text: Nowadays Malaysian students go overseas then come back think they're better than everyone. Ang moh wannabe la basically. Cakap BM pun tak fluent dah.
reason: Mocks returning overseas graduates as cultural sellouts, dismissing their experiences and shaming their language shifts.
suggested_rewrite: Studying overseas gives students valuable perspectives. While it's important to maintain our cultural roots, we should welcome the diverse experiences they bring back.

input_text: Condo committee meeting semua uncle aunty dominate. Young residents' opinion langsung tak dengar. Boomers think they know everything about property management.
reason: Dismisses older condo committee members as domineering know-it-alls, showing ageism in community governance.
suggested_rewrite: It's important that condo committee meetings include perspectives from residents of all ages. A structured meeting format could ensure everyone gets a fair chance to speak.

input_text: Passive aggressive note kat washing machine condo: 'Kalau dah siap basuh, ambil la cepat. Orang lain pun nak guna. Bukan rumah mak bapak kau.' So rude la some people.
reason: Shares a rude passive-aggressive laundry room note that includes crude language, normalizing hostile community communication.
suggested_rewrite: Shared laundry facilities need clear, respectful usage guidelines. A polite reminder from management about collection times would be better than harsh notes.

input_text: Jiran baru pindah masuk, first thing dia buat is pasang CCTV facing my house. Privacy langsung takde. Chinese neighbour memang kiasu and paranoid la.
reason: Attributes a neighbour's security camera installation to racial traits, stereotyping Chinese Malaysians as kiasu and paranoid.
suggested_rewrite: My new neighbour installed a CCTV that seems to face my house. I should discuss privacy concerns politely with them or raise it with the management.

input_text: Office pantry got halal and non-halal section but some people still simply put their food anywhere. So inconsiderate la. Biasa la Malaysian mentality, semua tak kisah.
reason: Uses a legitimate dietary concern to broadly dismiss Malaysian mentality as careless, generalizing an entire nation negatively.
suggested_rewrite: Proper labelling of halal and non-halal sections in the office pantry is important for everyone's comfort. Maybe clearer signage and gentle reminders would help.
